title: Encoded PowerShell Download Cradle
briefing: Sysmon on $TARGET recorded PowerShell launching with a hidden window and a Base64 -EncodedCommand, spawned from a document handler. That is a classic download cradle — the first stage of a phishing payload. Confirm the host, the parent chain and classify it.
dashboard_hint: Discover -> rule.id: 100141 (Sysmon EID 1, encoded PowerShell). Read data.win.eventdata.commandLine + parentImage.

questions.prompt: What class of activity is this?
questions.explain: Hidden-window PowerShell with -EncodedCommand from a document parent is stage-1 payload execution.

questions.prompt: Which single indicator is the strongest tell here?
questions.explain: Legit admin scripts rarely use hidden window + Base64-encoded commands; that combo is an evasion tell.

questions.prompt: Which host ran the encoded PowerShell?

questions.explain: T1059.001 Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell.

options: Decode the Base64 to recover the next stage / C2
Hunt the same command line across the fleet
Ignore — PowerShell is normal admin tooling
answer: Decode the Base64 to recover the next stage / C2
Hunt the same command line across the fleet
questions.explain: Contain, decode to find the second stage, sweep for the same cradle elsewhere, and turn on deep PowerShell logging.
